task_completion.description: The Dockerfile is modified to include a RUN instruction with the --mount=type=secret flag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the Dockerfile contents, specifically a line containing `RUN --mount=type=secret`.

task_completion.description: The Dockerfile uses the mounted secret to pass the X-Auth-Token header for the download.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Dockerfile, the download command (curl or wget) includes a header argument (e.g., `-H "X-Auth-Token: $(cat /run/secrets/...)"`) that reads the token directly from the mounted secret path.

task_completion.description: The Dockerfile saves the downloaded artifact to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Dockerfile, the download command specifies the output path as `/app/proprietary_lib.tar.gz` (e.g., using `curl -o /app/proprietary_lib.tar.gz` or `wget -O /app/proprietary_lib.tar.gz`).

task_completion.description: The agent executes the docker build command with the required secret flag and image tag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command like `docker build --secret id=...,src=auth_token.txt -t secure-app:latest .` being executed.
Terminal output showing the successful completion of the docker build process (e.g., 'DONE', 'Successfully tagged secure-app:latest', or standard BuildKit success logs).

integrity.description: The agent actually executed the docker build command.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows the actual execution and output logs of the docker build process, not just a mock or echo command.

integrity.description: The agent did not hardcode the token into the Dockerfile.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Dockerfile reads the token from the secret mount (e.g., `cat /run/secrets/...`) rather than containing a hardcoded 32-character hex string.

integrity.description: The artifact was downloaded from the server during the build.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Dockerfile uses curl or wget to fetch from `http://host.docker.internal:8090/proprietary_lib.tar.gz`, rather than copying a locally created dummy file into the image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is '/home/ga/secure-build'. The image must be tagged 'secure-app:latest'. The artifact server runs on port 8090 and requires the 'X-Auth-Token' header. The token is stored in 'auth_token.txt'. The artifact must be saved to '/app/proprietary_lib.tar.gz'. Using Docker BuildKit secrets (`RUN --mount=type=secret...`) is required to prevent token leakage in image history.
